codeReview.summary: AST-based context chunking implementation with ChunkScorer, AstChunkProvider, and ContextChunkingAssembler. All 19 tests pass. Code compiles cleanly with cargo check, clippy passes, and build succeeds.

issues.description: AstChunkProvider::parse_file silently returns empty Vec on parse error. While this prevents crashes, it could mask tree-sitter parsing failures. Consider logging the error or providing a way to distinguish 'no chunks found' from 'parse error'.

Unused variable `main_chunk` in test_build_context_with_dependency_graph. Similarly unused at lines 1000 (helper_chunk) and 1005 (deep_chunk). These are extracted but not used - consider removing or using them to verify deeper properties.
Unused variables `has_struct` and `has_impl` in test_chunk_type_in_scored_result. Extracted but not used to assert - the test only checks that scored_chunks is not empty.

summary: Feature implementation passes all verification criteria. The AST-based context chunking module provides intelligent chunking at function/class boundaries with relevance scoring using dependency graph analysis. Token budget enforcement is properly implemented. 19 tests cover scoring, chunking, and pipeline integration. No blocking issues found. Minor issues are test hygiene (unused variables) and silent error handling in parse_file that could be improved with logging.
